properties.name: Ghawar Oil Field
properties.significance: ~4% of global oil supply; largest conventional oil field on earth
properties.price_impact: Disruption would spike Brent $15–30/bbl minimum; no single asset has greater oil market impact
properties.alternative_supply: UAE spare capacity (3M bbl/day), Iraqi fields, strategic reserves (IEA 1.5B bbl)
properties.risk_factors: Missile/drone attack vulnerability (Abqaiq precedent); aging infrastructure (operational since 1948); Aramco IPO limits transparency

properties.name: Abqaiq Processing Facility
properties.significance: World's largest oil processing plant; stabilizes Saudi crude before export
properties.price_impact: Sept 2019 attack caused temporary 5.7M bbl/day production loss; Brent spiked 15% overnight
properties.alternative_supply: Saudi strategic reserves; can partially reroute through other terminals
properties.risk_factors: Proven drone/missile vulnerability — attacked by Houthis Sept 2019; single point of failure for Saudi oil exports

properties.name: Ras Tanura Terminal
properties.significance: Largest oil export terminal in the world
properties.price_impact: Closure would immediately cut global oil supply 6–7%; Brent +$20+/bbl
properties.alternative_supply: Jubail, Yanbu (Red Sea) terminals; pipeline to bypass Hormuz
properties.risk_factors: Gulf location vulnerable to Iranian action; Yanbu pipeline (3.2M bbl/day) can partially bypass Hormuz if needed

properties.name: North Dome / South Pars Gas Field
properties.capacity: Qatar: 77M tonnes LNG/year (world's largest exporter); Iran: ~10% of global gas reserves
properties.significance: World's largest single gas field; underpins Qatar's entire economy
properties.price_impact: Qatar LNG disruption would spike Asian LNG spot +30–50%; European gas +20–40%
properties.alternative_supply: Australia (Gorgon, Wheatstone), USA (Sabine Pass, Freeport), Russia LNG
properties.risk_factors: Shared field with Iran creates geopolitical tension; dependent on Hormuz for LNG exports

properties.name: Rumaila Oil Field
properties.operator: BP / CNPC (via contract with Basra Oil Company)
properties.significance: ~1.5% of global supply; largest oil field in Iraq
properties.price_impact: Disruption adds $5–10/bbl to Brent; Iraq produces 4.5M bbl/day total
properties.risk_factors: Militia activity near Basra; infrastructure neglect; water injection challenges; export dependent on Basra terminal

properties.name: Ahvaz / Khuzestan Oil Fields
properties.capacity: ~3.5M bbl/day gross potential; ~2.5M effective under sanctions
properties.significance: Iran's primary oil production region; ~3% of global supply
properties.price_impact: Sanctions removal would add 1–1.5M bbl/day to market; Brent -$5–10/bbl
properties.risk_factors: US/EU sanctions limit exports; covert exports via China, India, Syria; conflict with Israel risk; infrastructure decay

properties.name: Tengiz Oil Field
properties.significance: Major Western investment in Central Asia; second largest in CIS
properties.price_impact: CPC pipeline disruption (2022: 1M bbl/day cut) spiked Brent $3–5/bbl
properties.alternative_supply: Baku-Tbilisi-Ceyhan pipeline (partial); Russian routes create dependency risk
properties.risk_factors: Exports depend on CPC pipeline through Russia (single chokepoint); Russia has leverage

properties.name: Hassi Messaoud
properties.significance: Algeria's largest oil field; exports primarily to Europe
properties.price_impact: Moderate; Algeria supplies ~10% of European gas via Trans-Med pipeline
properties.alternative_supply: Libya, Nigeria, Middle East for European buyers

properties.name: Hassi R'Mel Gas Field
properties.capacity: 55 bcm/year peak; feeds 3 pipelines to Europe
properties.significance: Algeria's primary gas export hub; critical for Italy and Spain
properties.price_impact: Algeria supplies ~12% of European gas; disruption would pressure TTF prices +5–15%
properties.alternative_supply: Norwegian gas, LNG imports, Russian alternatives (political)

properties.name: Libyan Oil Fields (Sirte Basin)
properties.operator: NOC (National Oil Corporation) — various foreign partners
properties.significance: High-quality light sweet crude; critical to Mediterranean refiners
properties.price_impact: Libya production swings can move Brent $2–5/bbl; often cuts in line with political disputes
properties.alternative_supply: Algeria, Nigeria, North Sea for European buyers
properties.risk_factors: Ongoing civil conflict between east/west factions; frequent force majeure declarations; infrastructure used as political leverage

properties.name: Niger Delta Oil Fields
properties.operator: Shell, TotalEnergies, ExxonMobil, Chevron (JVs with NNPC)
properties.significance: Africa's largest oil producer; Bonny Light crude premium grade
properties.price_impact: Outages common; removal of fuel subsidy 2023 changed domestic dynamics
properties.alternative_supply: Angola, Equatorial Guinea, Middle East for European/Asian buyers
properties.risk_factors: Pipeline vandalism, oil theft (bunkering), militant activity, aging infrastructure, community conflicts

properties.name: North Sea Oil & Gas Fields
properties.operator: Equinor, Shell, BP, TotalEnergies and others
properties.capacity: UK: ~600k bbl/day; Norway: ~2M bbl/day oil equivalent
properties.significance: European domestic production; Brent crude benchmark pricing; Norwegian gas critical for Europe
properties.price_impact: North Sea maintenance shutdowns affect Brent dated; Norwegian gas strikes can spike TTF 10–20%
properties.alternative_supply: LNG imports, Russian pipeline alternatives (now limited), Algerian gas
properties.risk_factors: Mature basin — declining production; weather-dependent operations; labor strike risk (Norway)

properties.name: Permian Basin
properties.significance: World's most productive oil region; key to US energy independence and global swing supply
properties.price_impact: US shale acts as global supply buffer; Permian growth has capped oil prices structurally
properties.alternative_supply: Other US shale (Bakken, Eagle Ford); global spare capacity
properties.risk_factors: Water scarcity increasing; pipeline capacity constraints; regulatory environment

properties.name: Jamnagar Refinery Complex
properties.significance: India's refining hub; significant Russian crude processer since 2022
properties.price_impact: India as swing buyer of discounted Russian crude; Reliance helps set Asian refining margins
properties.risk_factors: Dependent on crude imports; increasing Russian crude share post-2022 creates geopolitical sensitivity

properties.name: Ulsan Refinery Hub
properties.significance: Northeast Asia's major refining hub; Saudi Aramco owns 17% of S-Oil
properties.price_impact: Heavily exposed to Middle East supply disruption; Hormuz closure is existential risk
properties.risk_factors: North Korea proximity; 100% import-dependent for crude; Saudi Aramco strategic partnership

properties.name: Zhoushan / Ningbo Refinery Hub
properties.significance: China's new private refining capacity; Zhejiang Petrochemical is one of world's newest mega-refineries
properties.price_impact: China's refining expansion affects Asian product markets; teapot refineries (Shandong) also significant
properties.risk_factors: Taiwan Strait tension; US sanctions on Chinese energy entities increasing

properties.name: Sabine Pass LNG Terminal
properties.significance: World's largest LNG export terminal; key to US LNG market emergence
properties.price_impact: Freeport explosion (2022) removed 20 MTPA for months — European TTF +15%; any US LNG outage now significant
properties.risk_factors: Hurricane risk; fire/explosion risk (Freeport 2022 precedent); US export policy risk

properties.name: Ras Laffan Industrial City
properties.significance: World's largest LNG export hub; anchor of Qatar's wealth
properties.price_impact: Any disruption would be catastrophic for global LNG markets; Asian JCC +$5–10/MMBtu
properties.risk_factors: Proximity to Hormuz; geopolitical position between Iran and Saudi Arabia; extreme concentration of output

properties.name: NW Shelf / Gorgon / Wheatstone LNG
properties.significance: Australia = world's largest or 2nd LNG exporter; key for Japan/Korea
properties.price_impact: Operational disruptions (cyclones, strikes) move Asian LNG spot prices; 2023 Chevron strikes raised JKM $1–2/MMBtu
properties.risk_factors: Cyclone exposure; worker strike risk; ageing facilities (NW Shelf)

properties.name: BTC Pipeline (Baku–Ceyhan)
properties.significance: Main export route for Caspian oil bypassing Russia and Iran
properties.price_impact: Key Azerbaijan/Kazakh export route; disruption affects European Caspian supply
properties.risk_factors: PKK attack history in Turkey; earthquake risk in Caucasus; 2008 Russia-Georgia war disrupted briefly

properties.name: Druzhba Pipeline
properties.significance: Historically the world's longest pipeline; supplied 25% of EU crude before 2022
properties.price_impact: EU embargo on Russian oil (Dec 2022) diverted most flows; Hungary/Slovakia still receive some; each disruption raises Ural-Brent spread
properties.risk_factors: Ukraine conflict; EU sanctions; Hungary as exception clause; Druzhba contamination incident (2019)

properties.name: Yamal LNG / Yamal Gas Fields
properties.capacity: 16.5 MTPA (Yamal LNG); Arctic LNG 2 under sanctions
properties.significance: Russia's main LNG export project; Arctic 2 under full Western sanctions
properties.price_impact: Western sanctions froze Arctic LNG 2; remaining Yamal LNG serves Asian buyers; reduces European supply options
properties.risk_factors: Full US/EU sanctions; tanker fleet constraints; ice-class vessel shortage; ice season limits

properties.name: Ukraine Gas Transit System
properties.operator: GTSOU (Gas Transmission System Operator of Ukraine)
properties.significance: Remaining gas transit corridor to Central/Eastern Europe; transit contract expired Jan 2025
properties.price_impact: Contract expiry Jan 2025 ended transit; Slovakia/Hungary/Austria most affected; TTF volatile on news
properties.risk_factors: Active war zone; contract expiry eliminated flows; Europe sought alternatives successfully

properties.name: Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Power Plant
properties.significance: Europe's largest nuclear plant; under Russian military control since March 2022
properties.price_impact: Nuclear incident would devastate European power markets; indirect impact on gas demand and European energy security
properties.risk_factors: Active conflict zone; repeated shelling; cooling system vulnerability; IAEA monitoring under constraints; nuclear safety incidents possible

properties.name: Singapore Oil Hub
properties.significance: Asia's primary oil trading and storage hub; benchmarks Asian crude pricing
properties.price_impact: Singapore storage inventory levels are Asian market indicator; Malacca closure would immediately impact
properties.risk_factors: Extreme Malacca Strait dependency; land scarcity limits expansion

properties.name: Port of Rotterdam / Energy Hub
properties.operator: Port of Rotterdam Authority; Shell, BP, Vitol, Trafigura terminals
properties.significance: European energy pricing benchmark; LNG import hub (Gate terminal); oil product distribution center
properties.price_impact: Rotterdam product cracks are global benchmark; ARA (Amsterdam-Rotterdam-Antwerp) inventory is key market indicator
properties.risk_factors: Climate flooding risk; transition away from fossil fuels strategy by 2050

properties.name: Houston Ship Channel / Gulf Coast Energy Hub
properties.operator: Multiple — Enterprise Products, Magellan, Philips 66, Valero
properties.capacity: Largest US energy export complex; major LNG, crude, products hub
properties.significance: US Gulf Coast is now world's largest crude exporter; prices WTI benchmark
properties.price_impact: Hurricane disruptions (Harvey 2017) spike WTI; Cushing OK inventory drives WTI futures
properties.risk_factors: Hurricane season (June–November); industrial accident risk; climate flooding
